market_briefing.summary: Global markets on March 20, 2026, rebounded due to easing Middle East tensions, although central banks remain cautious regarding inflation.
highlights: Indian benchmark indices (Nifty50, Sensex) surged, recovering from previous sell-offs on cooling crude oil prices and bargain hunting.
Brent crude prices eased to around $107 per barrel from earlier spikes, following signals of de-escalation in Middle East conflict.
Gold prices edged up but remain pressured by a strong US dollar and the Federal Reserve's hawkish stance.
The US market remains cautious, with analysts suggesting S&P 500 may not have bottomed amid 'hawkish hold' signals from the Fed.
Global central banks, including the ECB and Federal Reserve, maintain hawkish stances as energy shocks cloud the rate cut outlook.
